description: Analysis of offer use, timing, and effectiveness across all entries. Includes standard offer conditions, exceptional offers, and deadline psychology patterns.

default_rate.note: This is the default. Do not reduce without good reason. Opening position should always be 15%.

standard_offer.condition: EX-STL leads or high-value competitive situations where 15% is a stated barrier to signing
standard_offer.note: 13% is the tactical reduction from 15%. Still profitable. Use when 15% is the objection and lead quality justifies it.

exceptional_offer.condition: EXCEPTIONAL — SINGLE CASE IN ENTIRE PORTFOLIO
exceptional_offer.note: 12%+VAT was offered exactly once. Do not replicate without explicit Zac approval. This was an exceptional competitive situation with a high-value analytical-evaluator who pushed hard on fees.

deadline_psychology.conversion_evidence: Entry 019 Taj converted with a combination of income certainty (hospital proximity) AND a deadline offer. The deadline created urgency that closed the gap between interest and action.
how_to_use: Set a specific date, not a vague soon
Tie to something real — month end, quarter end, photography slot availability
State clearly what expires: the reduced rate returns to 15% after the date
Follow up the day before the deadline
when_to_use: Lead is clearly interested but not converting after 2+ follow-ups
Urgency-driven archetype who needs an external push
EX-STL who is comparing options and needs to decide
End of quarter when timing is natural
when_not_to_use: Certainty-seeker who will feel rushed and disengage
Betrayal-damaged who will feel pressured and lose trust
Lead who has not yet seen the full picture
First or second meeting — too early

GL-SELF.description: Self-managing and ready to hand over
GL-SELF.note: Lead has property and is already doing STR. Just needs to switch management. Fastest path to signing.

GL-MGMT.description: Currently with management company and ready to switch
GL-MGMT.note: Already committed to managed STR. Comparing providers. High conversion probability when differentiation is clear.

GL-TIME.note: Clear and near timeline. Property ready or nearly ready. Closing window is short — act fast or lose momentum.

converted_leads_summary.name: Gareth Howard
conversion_factors: existing Stayful client on another property
no fee objection from existing relationship

key_insight: Both converted leads were EX-STL profile. The highest conversion probability combination is EX-STL profile plus GL-MGMT green light. Deadline offer accelerates but trust and clear income gap are the primary drivers.
caution: Reducing the fee too early removes a future lever. Keep 15% as the standard opening position and treat reduction as a closing tool, not an opening position.
